sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.22 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: (J.\,G.\,Thompson). Let$G$be a finite group,$A$a group of automorphisms of$G$such that$|A|$and$|G|$are coprime. Does there exist an$A$-inva\-riant soluble subgroup$H$of$G$such that$C_A(H)=1$? { G.\,Glauberman} Yes, it does (S.\,A.\,Syskin, Siberian Math.~J., { 32}, no.\,6 (1991), 1034--1037).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
